title: Galium humilioides
intro: Galium humilioides, also known as Galium, is a plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, commonly referred to as a type of bedstraw or cleavers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of menopause. The bioactive constituents of Galium humilioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Galium humilioides is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, with the most common herbal preparations involving the whole plant or its dried leaves.
